content: We can hypothesize that microbial biological agents (natural autonomous organisms) evolve an intrinsic "curiosity module" analogous to the 2025 ICM framework for autonomous RL agents, rewarding them for exploring novel microenvironments and uncovering unutilized resources to improve their long-term survival fitness. This adapted ICM mechanism could be used to model and predict the spread of novel microbial biological agents in new, uncharted ecosystems like the human gut microbiome or remote soil habitats.
